summary: DeVault reviews his experience running postmarketOS on the Xiaomi Poco F1 as a daily driver replacement for his PinePhone. The initial setup is painful—requiring a Windows VM to unlock Xiaomi's bootloader, filesystem repairs, and significant Linux expertise—but once running, the phone performs far better than the PinePhone. Core features like telephony, SMS, mobile data, and general app usage work well, with cameras being the main non-functional hardware. He concludes this is the first mainline Linux phone experience he could recommend to others as a daily driver.
key_points: Xiaomi's bootloader unlocking process requires a Windows-only tool, a user account, phone number association, and a 30-day waiting period—hostile to free software users
Once past initial setup, the Poco F1 dramatically outperforms the PinePhone in base performance and general usability
Core phone functions (calls, SMS, mobile data) work well with minor audio profile bugs, while cameras remain completely non-functional
Waydroid provides promising but imperfect Android app compatibility, bridging the gap between Android and mainline Linux on mobile
The phone represents a milestone: the first mainline Linux mobile experience DeVault would recommend to others as a daily driver
money_quotes: The use of mainline Linux on a smartphone requires a certain degree of bullshit tolerance, and the main question is whether or not the bullshit exceeds your personal threshold.
This is very frustrating and far from the spirit of free software; Xiaomi earns few points for openness in my books.
I was required to apply a level of Linux expertise which exceeds that of even most enthusiasts to obtain a shell in the initramfs.
I transferred my personal data over from my PinePhone using a method which is 100% certifiably absent of bullshit, namely just rsyncing over my home directory.
It's not quite there yet, but Waydroid has a promising future and will do a lot to bridge the gap between Android and mainline Linux on mobile.
This phone, once the initial bullshit is addressed, is complete enough that it may be the first mainline Linux mobile experience that I might recommend to others as a daily driver.
key_insight: Despite a miserable initial setup process, the Xiaomi Poco F1 running postmarketOS crosses the threshold of daily-driver usability for mainline Linux phones, marking a significant milestone for the mobile Linux ecosystem.
